successCriteria.description: src/modules/policy/policy.model.ts exists and exports LeavePolicy, CreateLeavePolicyDto, UpdateLeavePolicyDto interfaces
src/modules/policy/policy.repository.ts exists and exports LeavePolicyRepository class implementing ILeavePolicyRepository
tests/unit/modules/policy/policy.repository.test.ts exists with Vitest tests covering all repository methods
All repository methods use the PostgreSQL pool from src/shared/db/connection.ts
All SQL queries use parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection
The SQL schema for leave_policies matches the canonical definition exactly
All repository methods follow the repository pattern (GP-001) and do not query the database directly from services

constraints: Repository pattern: All database access goes through repository interfaces (GP-001)
Input validation: Validate all inputs at API boundaries before processing (GP-003)
No sensitive data in logs: Never log passwords, tokens, PII, or financial data (GP-004)
Error handling: No unhandled promise rejections. All async errors are caught and handled (GP-006)
Modular monolith architecture: policy module must be self-contained
Dependency direction: policy module must not depend on leave, employee, balance, or notification modules
LeaveType must be imported from src/modules/leave/leave.model.ts
outOfScope: Any changes to existing modules outside src/modules/policy/

ambiguities.description: The intent mentions 'Jest unit tests' but the project uses Vitest. Should tests use Jest syntax or Vitest syntax?
options: Use Jest syntax (as mentioned in intent)

ambiguities.description: Should the repository throw errors when entities are not found (findById returns null vs throws)?
Should the repository use the shared pool import or receive Pool via dependency injection?
